overrides.override_summary: Franchisor entity (Hyatt Franchising, L.L.C.) unaudited balance sheet as of 12/31/2024 added to Item 21
overrides.why_it_matters: California requires franchisor-entity financials in addition to parent financials, providing transparency into the actual contracting entity's financial health

overrides.override_summary: Standard Hawaii franchise investment law disclosure. Filing does not constitute approval or recommendation. 7-day disclosure period. No waiver of reliance provision.
overrides.why_it_matters: Hawaii imposes minimum disclosure timing (7 days) and protects against waiver of franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Any release required as condition of renewal or transfer does not apply to liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees retain right to pursue claims under state franchise law even after signing a release at renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law but franchisor will enforce to extent enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisor's ability to terminate on insolvency grounds in Maryland

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring claims arising under Maryland Franchise Law in Maryland courts (subject to arbitration obligation), unless preempted by Federal Arbitration Act
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees need not travel to Chicago for Maryland Franchise Law claims

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law applies to claims arising under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Illinois choice of law for Maryland-specific franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Claims under Maryland Franchise Law must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: Establishes specific limitations period for Maryland franchise claims

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee must receive 90 days' notice of termination (with 60 days to cure) and 180 days' notice of non-renewal, per Minn. Stat. 80C.14
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer notice periods than standard FA provisions (which range from 24 hours to 30 days for most defaults)

overrides.override_summary: Any release as condition of renewal/transfer does not apply to extent prohibited by Minn. Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees retain right to pursue claims under Minnesota franchise law

overrides.override_summary: Minn. Rule Part 2860.4400J prohibits waiver of jury trial, waiver of procedure/forum/remedies, and consent to liquidated damages/termination penalties. Franchisor will enforce to extent law allows.
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota law may make the liquidated damages provisions unenforceable and preserves jury trial rights

overrides.override_summary: Minn. Stat. 80C.21 prohibits requiring litigation outside Minnesota. Franchisee retains right to submit matters to Minnesota courts (subject to arbitration obligation).
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees need not litigate in Illinois

overrides.override_summary: No action under Minn. Stat. 80C.17 more than 3 years after cause of action accrues
Commissioner has determined termination/liquidated damages to be unfair, unjust and inequitable within Section 51-19-09 of ND Franchise Investment Law. Parties will enforce to extent law allows.
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages provisions may be unenforceable in North Dakota — a critical protection for a $73M+ investment

overrides.override_summary: Any release does not apply to extent prohibited by applicable law for claims under ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees retain franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration at mutually agreed site (not necessarily Chicago). Franchisee may bring action in North Dakota courts (subject to arbitration).
overrides.why_it_matters: Significant relief from Chicago venue requirement

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law applies to the extent required by ND Franchise Investment Law
Waiver of punitive damages and jury trial may not be enforceable under ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves jury trial and punitive damages rights
